escalations.title: Mandatory ancillary benefits re-enrollment by 6/30 — never disclosed at signup

players.issueRole: Sent the June 12 open-enrollment notice; assigned benefit manager
Primary escalation point; confirmed the 6/30 lapse; tasked 5/15 to review contracts (no follow-up)
Issued the June 2 'non-responsive protocol' deadline (decision due by noon 6/3)
First asserted the 7/1 ancillary renewal, in March 2026
Confirmed medical 1/1; promised to confirm the ancillary date and never did
Executive looped in for the requested leadership call
Service operations contact on the account
Ran the Dec 2025 implementation/enrollment (effective 1/1/2026)
Pre-sale business consultant; sent master ancillary plan materials
Senior HR business partner on the account
CrossPeak's benefits broker (UNIQ); flagged the medical-vs-ancillary split
Raised the escalation on behalf of CrossPeak

escalations.summary: CrossPeak Solutions moved to Vensure (PEO) and completed benefits enrollment in December 2025, effective January 1, 2026. Vensure's 2026 Benefits Presentation described the coverage as the "2026 plan year," effective "January 1, 2026 through December 31, 2026." Beginning in March 2026 — roughly three months after enrollment — Vensure began telling CrossPeak that its ancillary benefits (dental, vision, life, disability, accident, and similar lines) actually run on a July 1–June 30 master plan year and would lapse on June 30, 2026 unless employees actively re-enrolled during a June 17–24 window. This July 1–June 30 ancillary cycle and the active re-enrollment requirement were never disclosed during sales or onboarding. Across roughly nine email threads over eight months, multiple Vensure representatives gave inconsistent answers or non-answers, and a documented commitment to review the contract for ancillary timelines went unanswered. The dispute over what CrossPeak was sold versus what Vensure now asserts remains open; CrossPeak has requested a call with Vensure leadership. On June 17, 2026, CrossPeak confirmed that the signed Benefit Election Contract does state coverage is effective through June 30, 2026 — a detail CrossPeak did not catch and that Vensure never pointed out; the core failure is that this was never surfaced during onboarding.

note.title: What we've confirmed — and where CrossPeak owns it
note.html:
Confirmed today (June 17, 2026): the signed Vensure Benefit Election Contract (BEC) does state that the elections and rates are "effective through June 30, 2026." In fairness, that language is in the document CrossPeak signed, and Zane did not catch it at signing.

The issue CrossPeak is raising is not that the date is missing — it is that no one at Vensure ever mentioned, flagged, or walked through the June 30 ancillary cutoff during sales, signing, or onboarding, while Vensure's own 2026 Benefits Presentation simultaneously described the coverage as the "January 1 – December 31, 2026 plan year." Surfacing a buried, consequential end date is exactly what a PEO's onboarding is supposed to do.

Paired with the onboarding failures documented in escalation #04 — where CrossPeak's policy and agreement templates were never requested or loaded — this reflects a significant onboarding miss on Vensure's part, not a one-off oversight.

impact: Roughly two weeks' notice on a hard deadline: ancillary coverage lapses June 30, 2026 if enrollment is not completed.
Risk that employees lose ancillary coverage through no fault of their own.
Vensure's own 2026 Benefits Presentation presented a January 1–December 31, 2026 plan year, contradicting the June 30 cutoff Vensure now asserts.
The July 1–June 30 ancillary cycle was never disclosed at sale or onboarding — it first surfaced in March 2026, about three months after enrollment.
Multiple Vensure representatives gave inconsistent or non-answers; a commitment to review the contract for ancillary timelines was never fulfilled.
Described by CrossPeak as the latest in a recurring pattern of service failures with Vensure.

timeline.text: Pre-sale: sends CrossPeak the master ancillary plan materials and a benefits enrollment demo.
Signs the Vensure benefit elections; coverage effective January 1, 2026.
Implementation recap to CrossPeak states 'Benefit Effective Date: 01/01/2026' for the full package (medical + all ancillary). No mention of a July 1 ancillary renewal.
CrossPeak completes open enrollment; all employee elections effective 1/1/2026.
Begins outreach about a 'July 1, 2026 benefit renewal.'
States 'All Vensure Master Plans renew on 7/1 annually' — the first assertion of a July 1 ancillary cycle, ~3 months after enrollment.
Notes the medical plan (Trustmark) is a 12-month contract renewing 1/1; identifies the 7/1 item as the ancillary lines.
Meeting recap; action item recorded: 'Brittany Shively to review contracts for timelines related to ancillary benefits.'
Notes Nate Schoenbrun 'believes we are covered until the end of the year' and will confirm.
Confirms the Master Medical effective date is 1/1; suggests reconnecting in September.
Asks directly: 'Are ancillary insurance options renewing in January as well?'
Says he is waiting to hear back from his team — and never provides a written answer.
Sends a 'Non-Responsive Protocol' notice: a decision is required by noon June 3, for a July 1 effective date.
Replies that CrossPeak signed up 'January 1 until next January 1,' expecting open enrollment in September.
Announces open enrollment: portal open June 17–24; all employees must elect or waive; elections effective July 1, 2026.
'Why am I receiving this?' — questions why an enrollment is being requested at all.
Clarifies the enrollment is for ancillaries, which run 7/1–6/30; major medical runs 1/1–12/30.
States no one knowledgeable has explained this; was repeatedly told coverage was good for the year; asks that someone take charge.
Confirms ancillaries will end 6/30 if no enrollment takes place.
Requests a call with Vensure's CEO and all parties; calls this one in a series of poor experiences.
Will connect with leadership to find available times. Executive Richard Heckner added to the thread.
Will compile this published record and review the signed agreements regarding the ancillary term.
Separately messaged Vensure's CEO directly on LinkedIn to request a meeting — in addition to asking Brittany Shively to schedule a leadership call — to ensure the request reaches leadership.

On reviewing the signed Benefit Election Contract, CrossPeak confirms it does state coverage is 'effective through June 30, 2026' — language CrossPeak did not catch at signing and that Vensure never flagged or explained during onboarding.

asks: Written confirmation of the ancillary benefits term CrossPeak agreed to, reconciled against Vensure's own 2026 Benefits Presentation (which states a January 1–December 31, 2026 plan year).
An explanation of why the July 1–June 30 ancillary cycle and the active re-enrollment requirement were not disclosed during sales or onboarding.
Given the late notice, that ancillary coverage be honored through December 31, 2026 as communicated — or that the June 30 lapse be waived and a no-cost extension granted.
A call with Vensure leadership — CEO included — with all current parties on copy.
references: Email cluster (Nov 2025 – Jun 2026), ~9 threads, incl.: "Action Requested: Open Enrollment Dates Available Now — CROSSPEAK SOLUTIONS — 45248"; "2026 Open Enrollment Crosspeak Solutions"; "Vensure — Medical 1/1"; "Meeting follow up"; "Crosspeak Solutions: Benefit Timeline".

Vensure participants: Jadiel Aponte (Benefits Manager), Brittany Shively (AVP Service Operations), Jenny Theriot (AVP Benefits), Kitisha Battle-Stimage (Benefit Specialist), Nate Schoenbrun (Client Growth Consultant), Brittany Taylor (Payroll Supervisor), Angel Deaton (Implementation), Brie Dixon (Business Consultant), Stella Anah (Sr. HR Business Partner), Richard Heckner (RVP Client Relations).
CrossPeak: Zane Perry (Partner). Broker: Nick Boeschen (UNIQ Benefit Solutions).

escalations.title: Payroll nearly missed and ran late — a first — right after Vensure swapped account resources with no handoff
escalations.status: Closed — payroll stabilized; no missed run since, thanks to Brittany Taylor

kudos.title: Corrective action — credit where it's due
kudos.html: Brittany Taylor turned this around. Since taking over CrossPeak's payroll she has not missed a single run, and has been responsive, reliable, and genuinely good to work with. The original miss was a broken hand-off, not her doing — and her steady follow-through is exactly the kind of ownership that rebuilds trust. Thank you, Brittany.

players.issueRole: Prior payroll/implementation lead; departed Vensure with no handoff — left only an auto-reply as payroll came due
New Service Operations Supervisor; later said she had 'recently just obtained this case' and was unsure if notes were left behind
AVP Service Operations; the April 13 reorg merged Client Relations + Payroll into Service Operations
CrossPeak payroll point; worked the late run on CrossPeak's side
Raised the escalation on behalf of CrossPeak

escalations.summary: On April 15, 2026 CrossPeak came within hours of missing a payroll — something that had never happened in the company's history — and ultimately ran payroll late that day. The timing was not a coincidence: Vensure's internal reorganization merging Client Relations and Payroll into a single 'Service Operations' team took effect April 13, two days earlier. CrossPeak's established payroll contact (Amiee Caldwell) was gone with only an auto-reply, and the newly assigned supervisor later acknowledged she had 'recently just obtained this case' and did not know whether any notes had been left behind. Vensure described a 'stuck approval workflow' as the cause, but that explanation was given only by phone/Slack and never put in writing, and no formal root-cause analysis or written fee-reimbursement commitment has been provided.
impact: First near-missed and ultimately late payroll in CrossPeak's history — direct risk to employees being paid on time.
Caused by a resource swap with no advance notice and no warm handoff: the prior payroll lead was gone and the replacement had just received the account.
Vensure's stated root cause (a 'stuck approval workflow') exists only verbally — no written RCA, so there is no documented assurance it cannot recur.
No written confirmation of any fee reimbursement/credit for the disruption — only informal discussion.
Erodes trust in the most time-critical function a PEO performs.

timeline.text: Vensure reorganization merging Client Relations and Payroll into a single 'Service Operations' team takes effect — two days before payroll is due.
CrossPeak's established payroll contact is gone; outreach returns only an auto-reply as payroll comes due.
Payroll nearly missed; after escalation it is processed LATE that day — the first late payroll in CrossPeak's history.
Newly assigned supervisor; later states she 'recently just obtained this case' and is unsure whether the prior lead left any notes.
Attributes the delay to a 'stuck approval workflow' and promises a root-cause review — conveyed only by phone/Slack, never in writing.
Requests a written root-cause analysis and confirmation of a fee credit for the disruption.
Marks this resolved: payroll has been stable with no missed runs since, and Brittany Taylor has been reliable and great to work with. Closing with thanks to her for the turnaround.

asks: A written root-cause analysis of the late payroll and the specific controls Vensure has put in place so it cannot recur.
Written confirmation of the fee reimbursement/credit for the disruption (not just a verbal discussion).
A single, stable, named payroll owner — and a backup — for CrossPeak's account.
A commitment to give CrossPeak advance notice and a documented warm handoff before any change to the people who run its payroll.
references: Email thread 'Payroll' / late-run escalation (April 2026); Vensure 'Service Operations' reorg announcement (effective 4/13/2026).
Vensure participants: Amiee Caldwell (former payroll lead), Brittany Taylor (Service Operations Supervisor), Brittany Shively (AVP Service Operations). CrossPeak: Zane Perry, Natalie Christie.
Note: a small Vensure invoice ($152.56, dated 2/13/2026) was also left ~91 days past due.

escalations.title: Multi-state tax registration and access never completed after the PEO migration — unresolved ~37 weeks
escalations.status: Open — tax working session held 6/10; South Carolina agent-role conflict still unresolved

players.issueRole: Onboarding business consultant; first raised the unfinished state tax-ID registrations (3/2); onboarding did not complete them
AVP Service Operations; primary escalation point; her 5/14 status reply omitted California
Service Operations Supervisor; day-to-day coordinator; scheduled the 6/10 tax call
SVP Payroll Tax; joined the 6/10 call; clarified SC agent roles
Unemployment Claims Specialist; collected the SC/IL/GA/CA unemployment POAs (5/1–5/5)
Senior HR Business Partner; named to the cross-functional account team
Regional VP, Client Relations; relationship-level escalation contact
GDOL staff (external); directed the Georgia DOL-1N reactivation path
Raised and tracked the escalation, and personally regained access to each state portal

escalations.summary: When CrossPeak migrated from Engage PEO to Vensure in late 2025, Vensure did not complete CrossPeak's state employment-tax registrations and access in four states — South Carolina, California, Georgia, and Illinois. It surfaced in March 2026, then stalled on a recurring blocker: CrossPeak's FEIN already had existing state accounts, so Vensure could not register new ones and instead needed either CrossPeak's portal logins or third-party-agent authority via signed POAs. Over the following months CrossPeak signed every POA requested and personally regained access to each state portal, while repeatedly asking Vensure to name one accountable owner and to hold a working call with its tax lead. By mid-May the items were in their 34th week; Zane requested a call three times between 5/27 and 6/8 with none scheduled, and Vensure's 5/14 status update omitted California entirely. A tax-team call finally occurred 6/10. As of 6/17, South Carolina filing access is still blocked by an unresolved agent-role conflict in the state portal.
impact: Four states (SC, CA, GA, IL) without confirmed Vensure tax access for roughly 37 weeks after migration — risk of late or missed employer state tax filings for Q1–Q2 2026.
Potential penalty/interest exposure; CrossPeak has no written confirmation of filing status per state or of who covers penalties under the Client Service Agreement.
CrossPeak (the client) had to do the recovery work itself — regaining portal access, chasing the prior PEO and the states, and maintaining its own tracker.
Three documented requests for a working call (5/27, 6/1–6/2, 6/5–6/8) went unscheduled; a 6/3 reply asked for credentials and ignored the call request.
California was omitted from Vensure's 5/14 written status update despite Vensure holding the EDD credentials and TPA data since 4/30.
As of 6/17 South Carolina roles cannot be assigned because the SUITS portal allows one agent per role and Vensure's claims agent conflicts with its payroll-tax agent.

timeline.text: States Vensure still needs to apply for CrossPeak's state tax IDs and asks for per-state start dates and whether Engage restored portal access.
Reports CA sent to the tax team and GA/SC intake forms sent: 'Now we wait.'
Key blocker: for SC, IL and GA the FEIN already has a state account, so Vensure 'cannot register' — CrossPeak must provide logins or use the POA/TPA route.
Reports regaining California (EDD) access; gives a state-by-state status; requests daily meetings with a tax lead and to be added as a user on every state account.
Meets with Shawn Helie and signs/submits all requested POAs (SC, GA, IL, CA DE-48).
Sends a Leadership Alignment request: state items in their 34th week, no clear owner; asks for a named lead to drive completion.
Replies that she is aware of IL, SC and GA login needs — California not mentioned — and will coordinate a team meeting.
Follows up: 'What remains?' — no reply received.
Replies asking for the MyTax Illinois credentials; does not address the request for a call with the tax lead.
Notes 'we have done a full circle,' that items are in their 37th week, and that he has asked for a call three times in a week and a half with none scheduled.
Joins the working call (with both Brittanys) to address SC, IL and GA.
Explains again that the SC SUITS portal will not allow overlapping delegates for two organizations and asks how to proceed.
Clarifies which SC agent handles claims vs. payroll tax — but does not resolve the SUITS one-agent-per-role conflict.

asks: Written confirmation of CrossPeak's Q1 and Q2 2026 state tax filing status in all four states — what is filed, what is late, and who covers any penalties or interest under the Client Service Agreement.
Resolve the South Carolina SUITS role conflict in writing so the Wage and Payment Submission roles can be assigned and overdue filings cleared.
A single, named, accountable owner for CrossPeak's state-tax matters, and confirmation that California EDD TPA has been established (it was omitted from the 5/14 status).
Confirm Illinois and Georgia access are fully established and close out each state.
Add Zane as a user on every state account going forward, and explain why these registrations were not completed during onboarding.
references: CrossPeak-maintained 'Vensure State Registration — Open Issues Tracker' (Google Doc).

Email cluster Mar–Jun 2026: 'State registrations'; Engage account list; 'POAs Needed for Vensure TPA — Unemployment'; 'California — Access granted!'; 'Request for Leadership Alignment Meeting'; the 6/10 recap and GDOL DOL-1N threads.
Matter IDs: SC DEW Employer ID 10010600 (agents SC9Z6Y, SC8P2J, SC9W1Q); CA EDD 116 7722 6; GDOL 399211-02; IL POA Agent ID VENT001.
Vensure participants: Brie Dixon, Brittany Shively, Brittany Taylor, Misty Ramsey, Shawn Helie, Stella Anah, Richard Heckner. External: Velzie Martin (GDOL); Engage PEO (prior provider).

escalations.title: CrossPeak's account was never properly onboarded — new-hire policy and agreement templates were never requested or loaded
escalations.status: Open — templates delivered 6/16; Vensure quotes 2+ weeks to load

players.issueRole: Senior HR Business Partner; author of the 'Vensure does not create templates' and '2+ weeks' statements
Service Operations Supervisor; payroll/system POC and onboarding routing
AVP Service Operations; the 5/15 responsibilities outline assigned no owner for template creation
Ran the Dec 2025 implementation, whose 'Items Needed' list was tax/payroll only — the scope gap at its source
New hire (started 6/15) who received nothing from Vensure to sign
New hire (started 6/15) who received nothing from Vensure to sign
Raised the company-documents question repeatedly since January; rebuilt all templates overnight

escalations.summary: When CrossPeak migrated from Engage to Vensure, Vensure's implementation scope covered only tax and payroll setup — it never requested CrossPeak's existing-employee hire paperwork or its new-hire policy, handbook, and agreement templates, and none were ever loaded into the onboarding workflow. Zane raised the question of company onboarding documents repeatedly from January onward, and the May 15 'responsibilities' outline assigned no owner for template creation. The gap surfaced concretely on Monday 6/15/2026 when two new hires — Ty Mitchell and Olivia Small — started and received nothing to sign. Only on 6/16 was CrossPeak first told in writing that the finished templates had to come from CrossPeak; CrossPeak rebuilt them overnight and delivered them. On 6/17 Vensure said the new templates will take its Client Solutions team '2+ weeks' to process, and separately ruled that NDAs, non-competes, confidentiality, arbitration, and employment-contract documents are not permitted in the onboarding workflow at all.
impact: Two new hires started 6/15/2026 with no CrossPeak policy, handbook, or agreement documents to sign — no executed acknowledgements for either.
Records gap: CrossPeak's signed employee-document library was never carried across PEO migrations (Engage never handed it over; Vensure never requested it).
CrossPeak had to recreate its template set from scratch overnight under time pressure — work that should have been part of implementation.
Even after delivery, Vensure quotes 2+ weeks to load the documents, extending the period new hires go unsigned.
Vensure rejected NDAs, non-compete, confidentiality, arbitration, and employment-contract documents from the onboarding workflow — a process limitation disclosed only after the fact.
Day-one experience degraded: a new hire repeatedly reported receiving nothing and being unsure whether he had system access.

timeline.text: Implementation welcome call. The 'Items Needed' list is entirely tax/payroll — no request for existing-employee hire paperwork or new-hire policy/handbook templates.

Leadership Alignment email: 'After approximately four months of asking about onboarding templates and historical HR documents being loaded… This should have been addressed during the initial onboarding phase.'
Post-meeting responsibilities outline — no action item for onboarding-template creation is assigned to anyone.
Reports new hire Ty has received nothing from Vensure; asks Vensure to confirm both new hires are in the onboarding process.
'What about the employment agreement and all other policy documents every employee signs?… there was an action to get all policies and employee agreements loaded into the system.'

First clear written statement of ownership: 'Vensure does not create onboarding document templates. However, you are welcome to provide the specific templates you would like included…' — after the hires had already started.
Delivers the finalized, rebuilt template set to Vensure for loading — rebuilt from the document set Engage previously had every new hire sign.
'It will likely take the Client Solutions team 2+ weeks to add the documents to onboarding due to the volume and customization required.'
Rules that NDAs, confidentiality, non-compete, arbitration, and employment-contract documents are not permitted in the onboarding workflow and must be sent separately with the offer letter.

asks: Acknowledge in writing that CrossPeak's existing-employee paperwork and new-hire templates were never requested during implementation, and confirm the corrected onboarding scope going forward.
Expedite loading the delivered templates well inside the quoted '2+ weeks' — commit to a firm date — given two hires are already onboarded with nothing to sign.
Provide a written, owner-assigned onboarding checklist (who provides what, by when) for implementation and for each future new hire, so this cannot recur.

Confirm the compliant path for the documents Vensure excludes from the workflow (NDA, confidentiality, non-compete, arbitration, employment contracts) and how Vensure will support sending them at offer time.
Confirm the two new hires' statutory items (I-9 / E-Verify) are complete or on a tracked deadline, independent of the template backlog.
references: Email threads: 'New Hire Onboarding — Ty Mitchell & Olivia Small (starting 6/15)'; 'CrossPeak Onboarding Policies & Employee Handbook — please load and send for signature'; 'Request for Leadership Alignment Meeting'; 'Meeting follow up' (5/15 responsibilities).
Drive: the rebuilt onboarding template set (created 6/16–6/17/2026); the Nov 2025 Implementation Welcome Call deck ('Items Needed' = tax/payroll only); Engage/Vensure 'Open Issues' notes.
Vensure participants: Stella Anah, Brittany Taylor, Brittany Shively, Amiee Caldwell, plus PrismHR platform contacts. New hires referenced by name and role only (no personal data).

escalations.title: 401(k) breakdown after migration — missing statements and loan-repayment deductions not taken
escalations.status: Closed — resolved directly with the 401(k) / Slavic team

kudos.title: Resolved — thank you to the team
kudos.html: Donna Hudson was able to work directly with the Slavic401k / PrismHR team to restore her access, get her statements, and correct the missed loan-repayment deductions. Thank you to the team for stepping in and supporting Donna all the way to a clean resolution.

players.issueRole: CrossPeak employee directly impacted — lost 401(k) access, no statements, loan-repayment deductions not taken
Assigned 401(k) point of contact who lacked the credentials to answer
PrismHR client-service contact in the handoff chain
Escalated on behalf of the affected employee

escalations.summary: After CrossPeak migrated to Vensure, a CrossPeak employee (Donna Hudson) lost access to her 401(k) information, received no statement of any postings since the transfer, and — most seriously — found that loan-repayment deductions were not being taken for multiple pay periods, throwing off her payoff schedule. The recordkeeper is Slavic401k, administered through the PrismHR platform. The assigned point of contact did not have the credentials needed to answer basic questions, leaving the employee without a clear path to resolution.
impact: Loan-repayment deductions not taken for multiple pay periods — a payroll-accuracy and fiduciary concern, not just an access annoyance.
No 401(k) statements or visibility into postings since the transfer began, so the employee cannot verify her own account.
The assigned point of contact lacked credentials to answer, with no clear escalation path provided.
Undermines employee trust in retirement administration during the PEO transition.

timeline.text: Reports she cannot access any 401(k) information since the move to Vensure, has no statement of postings since the transfer, and that deductions have not been taken for many pay periods, throwing off her goal.
Escalates to Vensure and asks who can answer for the Slavic401k account and correct the missed deductions.
Assigned as 401(k) POC but does not have the credentials to access the account and answer the questions.
Surfaces in the contact-handoff chain for the Slavic/PrismHR 401(k) platform.
Worked directly with the Slavic401k / PrismHR team to restore access, obtain statements, and correct the missed loan-repayment deductions — issue resolved.

asks: Confirm in writing whether loan-repayment deductions were missed, for which pay periods, and the plan to correct the schedule and any make-up contributions.
Provide the employee's full 401(k) statements/postings since the transfer and restore her account access.
Name a 401(k) point of contact who actually has the credentials and authority to resolve account questions.
Reconcile the account and confirm contributions and loan balances are accurate going forward.
references: Slack DM (Zane ↔ affected employee, 4/22/2026): could not access 401(k) since moving to Vensure; no statement of posts; deductions not taken for many pay periods.
Email 'Meeting follow up' and PrismHR contact handoff; recordkeeper Slavic401k via the PrismHR platform.
Participants: Donna Hudson (CrossPeak), Bracken El-Bakri and James Kaiser (PrismHR/Slavic). No account numbers or personal data included.

escalations.title: Chronic turnover and no single account owner — repeated reassignments with no warm handoff
escalations.status: Closed — Brittany Shively driving escalations; Brittany Taylor anchoring the account, absent an assigned account manager

kudos.title: Who showed up
kudos.html: Brittany Taylor has effectively served as CrossPeak's primary point of contact — despite being titled Payroll Supervisor — and has supported the account well. Brittany Shively has actively backed escalations and recently apologized to CrossPeak directly for how a recent call was handled. In a rough situation, both have been the bright spots. Thank you.

players.issueRole: Onboarding business consultant — has since left Vensure (now at Paychex)
Payroll/implementation lead — departed Vensure with no handoff (see the payroll near-miss)
Took over mid-stream having 'recently just obtained this case'
AVP Service Operations after the Client Relations + Payroll reorg
Introduced as a brand-new 'Client Growth Consultant' months into the relationship
HR Business Partner — not identified to CrossPeak until ~May, months in
Regional VP, Client Relations; relationship-level escalation contact
Had to reverse-engineer his own support team and repeatedly ask 'who is my contact?'

escalations.summary: Across the relationship CrossPeak has cycled through a rotating cast of Vensure contacts who left or were reassigned mid-stream with no warm handoff, and never received a proper account kickoff. CrossPeak repeatedly had to ask who its official contacts even were — for HR, for payroll, for the account overall. Zane tied the April payroll near-miss directly to the 'improper offboarding of Amiee Caldwell and insufficient onboarding of Brittany Taylor.' Contacts have continued to churn: the business consultant who onboarded the account has since left Vensure entirely. The pattern is the root cause behind several of the other escalations on this log. This is being closed in recognition of the individuals who have shown up for CrossPeak — not because the underlying instability is resolved: CrossPeak still has no formally assigned account manager, and a Payroll Supervisor has had to fill that gap.
impact: No proper kickoff and no single accountable owner — CrossPeak has had to reconstruct its own support roster.
Reassignments happened with no warm handoff, directly contributing to the late payroll and the stalled state registrations.
Key contacts departed mid-engagement (Amiee Caldwell; Brie Dixon, now at a competitor), taking institutional knowledge with them.
New contacts were introduced months into the relationship, restarting context each time.
Repeated 'who is my contact?' requests reflect time lost to chasing ownership instead of resolving issues.
No official account manager has ever been assigned to CrossPeak — a Payroll Supervisor has had to fill the role unofficially.

timeline.text: Notes there is still no official HR contact identified for the account, months into the relationship.
'Handoff' email: 'This has been an absolute nightmare of an experience… Who is my official HR contact moving forward?'
Introduced as a brand-new 'Client Growth Consultant' — a new face months into the engagement.
Reorg merges Client Relations and Payroll into 'Service Operations'; CrossPeak's payroll contact changes with no advance notice (precipitating the 4/15 late payroll).
Confirms via LinkedIn that the onboarding business consultant (Brie Dixon) has left Vensure for a competitor — the latest example of contact instability.
Apologizes to CrossPeak directly for how the open-states progress call was handled; the tax lead (Misty Ramsey) acknowledged she had not been prepared for that call.
Notes CrossPeak still has no officially assigned account manager — Brittany Taylor (titled Payroll Supervisor) has filled that role unofficially and supported the account well.
Closing this as actively supported — but the underlying instability that caused it is real and remains documented across the open escalations.

asks: Assign one named, accountable owner for the CrossPeak account, with a named backup.
Provide a written escalation path (who, and to whom, when an item stalls).
Commit to advance notice and a documented warm handoff before any change to CrossPeak's key contacts.
Hold the proper account review/kickoff that never occurred, to re-baseline open items and ownership.
references: Email 'Handoff' (5/11/2026) and 'Introducing Your Client Growth Consultant' (5/11–5/14/2026).
State-registration and payroll threads documenting the lack of a single owner and the reorg-driven contact change.
LinkedIn: Brie Dixon now lists Paychex as her employer (formerly Vensure).
